title: Anthropic teams up with Goldman Sachs and Blackstone on $1.5B AI venture to deploy Claude across private equity-owned firms
description: Wall Street is stepping deeper into AI, and this time it’s not just about funding models. It’s about putting them to work inside real businesses. title: No systemic risk to BFSI sector; may see short-term slowdown in loan growth: Deepak Parekh
description: Industry veteran Deepak Parekh stated the banking and financial services sector is not facing major systemic risks from global geopolitical uncertainties. He anticipates a short-term slowdown in loan growth. Real estate investment trusts are growing in India. Insurance penetration remains low. Deposit mobilization has moderated as retail investors favor mutual funds. Artificial intelligence's impact on finance is still evolving.
content:

title: AI Firms to Give US Government Early Access for Model Evaluation
description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google, Microsoft Corp. and xAI have agreed to give the US government early access to their artificial intelligence models to assess the systems’ capabilities and help improve their security before the technology is released to the public.

title: Cognizant May Cut Up To 15,000 Jobs Globally, India To Be Most Impacted
description: Cognizant may cut between 12,000 and 15,000 jobs globally as part of a major restructuring exercise, with most of the impact expected in India. The possible layoffs are linked to the company’s internal transformation programme aimed at shifting towards an artificial intelligence (AI)-focused business model
content:
Cognizant may cut between 12,000 and 15,000 jobs globally as part of a major restructuring exercise, with most of the impact expected in India, Moneycontrol reported.

title: Coinbase to Cut 14% of Staff, Citing Volatile Markets and AI
description: Crypto exchange Coinbase Global Inc. will cut around 14% of its workforce, citing a need to manage costs in volatile markets and technological advances in artificial intelligence.

title: Crypto exchange Coinbase to cut about 14% of workforce
description: Coinbase said on Tuesday it will cut about 700 jobs, or about 14% of its global ​workforce, as part of a restructuring plan aimed at ‌reducing costs and repositioning the business for the artificial intelligence era.
content:
The move comes as the crypto exchange navigates volatility in crypto markets. ​Layoffs have been widespread across U.S. companies at the ​start of the year, as businesses cut expenses, simplify ⁠operations and adjust to the growing use of AI ​tools.
